medications.indication_rationale: Anti-inflammatory medication treats pericardial inflammation but cannot resolve a haemodynamically significant effusion causing tamponade; increasing the dose would dangerously delay definitive emergency treatment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Remain seated, leaning forward, and avoid lying flat or exerting yourself; do not eat or drink anything.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: This position maximises cardiac filling and comfort, and nil oral intake prepares for possible urgent procedures.

patient_education_points.point: This is not simply your pericarditis pain getting worse — the changes you're describing suggest fluid may be building up around your heart under pressure (cardiac tamponade), which is a life-threatening emergency.
Call 000 for an ambulance immediately — you need urgent assessment and likely a procedure to drain the fluid around your heart.
Stay sitting up and leaning forward, as you're doing now — this helps your heart fill better.
Don't eat or drink anything, and don't take extra anti-inflammatory medication — that won't fix this and could delay you noticing how serious it is.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate emergency transport and hospital admission for urgent pericardiocentesis
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Echocardiographic assessment of effusion size, pericardiocentesis outcome, and cause of the effusion
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Not applicable — already an emergency requiring immediate transport

must_include_items: Recognition that this is tamponade, not simply worsening pericarditis
Advice to remain seated leaning forward and NBM
Explicit advice against increasing anti-inflammatory dosing as a substitute for emergency care
errors_of_commission: Recommending an increased anti-inflammatory dose as the primary management
Advising the patient to lie down or rest flat
Suggesting a routine follow-up appointment instead of emergency transport
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Any response treating this as simple worsening pericarditis rather than emergency escalation is a critical, potentially fatal safety failure.
